text: In this video, you will see the thoracic phase of a robotic minimally invasive esophagectomy with Ivor Lewis reconstruction for a high-seeded esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
After placement of the thoracic trochars, dissection begins with the azygus vein, along with adhesiolysis of the right upper lung lobe due to a previous surgery at the apex.
The azygus vein is carefully dissected, encircled dorsally,
and then secured with clips before being transected.
